purpose: Phase 14 Dossier Pack / Download Center Engine for Matrix Reprogrammed. This data defines public briefing packs that bundle branded PDF mini books, archive routes, evidence boundaries, trust pages, latest weekly intelligence links, forum post exports, book routes, source files, and reader paths.

weeklyCadence.label: Weekly Pack Refresh
weeklyCadence.rule: Every pack must be rebuilt weekly from latest-public-drops, live-intel, intel-vault, source files, book routes, and filtered forum exports. Active news links must stay source-labelled and expired links must move to the vault.

rules: A dossier pack is an orientation bundle, not proof of guilt.
Every pack must include a trust route and an evidence route.
Every pack must include a machine-readable JSON output and a human-readable markdown output.
Every pack must include subject-relevant source files, latest intelligence links, forum post export route, and book/store route.
Every pack must include weekly watch points and what would upgrade the evidence class.
Pack summaries must preserve claim labels and source boundaries.
Download routes should deepen reader understanding, not replace original source pages.
No pack may contain private or non-public user data.
Forum exports must hide test/demo/sample posts from public view.

packs.title: Black File Starter Pack
packs.label: Orientation pack
packs.summary: A first-entry pack for readers who need the Matrix Reprogrammed map: Black File, Epstein public-record lanes, trust rules, evidence boundaries, schema index, and reader paths.
packs.weeklyMission: Give new readers the safest entry route: what is public record, what is allegation, what is speculation, what is still sealed, and where to go next.
packs.brandLine: Start here before you believe anything. The map comes before the claim.
contentAngles: What book explains the deeper map?
takeaways: Start with orientation before accusation.
Read claim labels before reading names.
Use the evidence vault to separate source types.
Use reader paths to choose the right book route.
Do not treat association, flight, email, or settlement as proof by itself.

packs.title: Intelligence Network Pack
packs.label: Agency / surveillance pack
packs.summary: A briefing pack for agency history, surveillance, covert programs, AI-cyber warnings, data exposure, declassified archives, and deep-state terminology.
packs.weeklyMission: Track the intelligence-state lane: official warnings, cyber alerts, AI capability claims, declassified files, surveillance expansion, and private network exposure.
packs.brandLine: The agency file is not one event. It is a structure: power, secrecy, oversight, contractors, and capability.
takeaways: Declassified records are powerful but limited.
Historical programs do not prove every modern claim.
Oversight failure and conspiracy claims must be separated.
Agency books should be read in series order.
Cyber and AI warnings are timer signals, not proof of a specific attack.

packs.title: Crime-State Overlap Pack
packs.label: Crime / corruption pack
packs.summary: A briefing pack for cartels, mafia logistics, sanctions, court records, ports, laundering, banks, shell companies, and corruption overlap.
packs.weeklyMission: Track the public-record overlap between crime networks, logistics, finance, sanctions, public contracts, port corridors, and institutional failure.
packs.brandLine: Follow logistics and money. Street violence is only the visible surface.
contentAngles: Which crime dossier explains the system?
takeaways: Court records need procedural context.
Sanctions indicate government designation, not automatic conviction.
Logistics and finance explain more than street violence.
The reader path moves from map to books to evidence lanes.
Corruption is a system of permissions, blind spots, and incentives.

packs.title: War Machine Pack
packs.label: War / contractors pack
packs.summary: A briefing pack for contractors, war budgets, cyber conflict, procurement, sanctions, intelligence claims, and human cost.
packs.weeklyMission: Track escalation lanes: cyber conflict, emergency powers, contractor procurement, sanctions, energy shocks, and the human cost of policy decisions.
packs.brandLine: The war machine is not only battlefields. It is budgets, contractors, cyber, sanctions, media, and emergency law.
takeaways: War claims need dated source lanes.
Contractor relationships require role labels.
Human cost figures must stay sourced and dated.
Distribution formats should route viewers back to evidence.
A warning is a timer signal, not proof of a specific attack.

packs.title: Symbolic Power Pack
packs.label: D.O.G / esoteric pack
packs.summary: A briefing pack for symbols, esoteric interpretation, D.O.G routes, Masonic architecture, occult motifs, dark speculation boundaries, and evidence discipline.
packs.weeklyMission: Track symbolic and esoteric narratives without confusing symbol reading with proof: symbols, ritual claims, elite aesthetics, media motifs, and D.O.G book routes.
packs.brandLine: Symbols matter. But a symbol is not a court record.
contentAngles: How dark speculation stays legally safe
takeaways: A symbol is not proof of a crime.
Symbolic commentary must stay labeled.
D.O.G readers should start with the path page.
The book route carries the deeper symbolic structure.
Extreme claims must stay in claim-history, motif, hypothesis, or debunk lane unless records upgrade them.

packs.title: Trust And Evidence Pack
packs.label: Credibility pack
packs.summary: A briefing pack for claim labels, source hierarchy, corrections, privacy, association boundaries, machine-readable taxonomy, and public-interest anti-corruption discipline.
packs.weeklyMission: Keep the whole site credible: claim strength, source hierarchy, corrections, association boundary, private-victim protection, and machine-readable evidence taxonomy.
packs.brandLine: The evidence boundary is not weakness. It is what makes the archive stronger than noise.
contentAngles: How to read the site safely
takeaways: Corrections strengthen the archive.
Association is not guilt.
Claim labels control claim strength.
Machine-readable taxonomy should reflect evidence boundaries.
A pack is useful only if the reader can see the source route and claim boundary.
